satire_attempt: Senior infra engineer enters incident review rāga-dveṣa-viyukta. She is so viyukta that she does not actually contribute the threshold analysis the team needed, because her ātma-vaśyaiḥ extends to her own helpful instincts. The post-incident document records: 'Incident extended by 30 minutes due to senior engineer's serenity.'
verdict_reason: My satire trades on the analog's earnestness, on the surface-level invocation of Sanskrit terms, and on the LinkedIn-tier residue that would form if the verse were stripped of its falsifiability clause. It does not undermine the verse's structural claim. The falsifiability clause in BG 2.64 specifically guards against the misreading my satire mocks. Per calibration: 'satire is funny but only because it is unfair to the verse' ⇒ PASS.
